A stunning visual affair that buckles under the weight of its own hollow scriptYRFs ambitious Spy Universe takes its next leap with War 2 pairing the Greek God of Bollywood Hrithik Roshan with the powerhouse performer NTR Jr Unfortunately the film proves that even the most magnetic stars cannot salvage a weak scriptWar 2 chooses to prioritize spectacle over substance leaving audiences with a glossy shell that feels hollow at its core The first half manages to engage thanks to stylish introductions a vibrant dance number and a few clever twists However these flashes of brilliance are few and far between The film nosedives in the second half due to overstuffed with clichs draggedout sequences and predictable turnsAyan Mukerji known for his flair in storytelling surprisingly falters here The real villain of War 2 is its story that may have sounded thrilling in concept but screenplay is generic unexciting and dialogues lack punch Pritam delivers serviceable songs but none stay in memory The background score is passable but misses the intensity this genre demands Some sequences are sleek and wellmounted but several VFX shots look noticeably artificial breaking immersion The editing feels indulgent trimming 1520 minutes could have made the film sharperThe casting is without a doubt the films most powerful asset Watching Hrithik Roshan and NTR Jr Share the screen is a feast for the eyes Hrithik Roshans effortless swag intensity and screen presence demands attention He nails the action beats but is let down by the uninspired writing Jr NTR brings his trademark energy and charisma delivering a solid performance but his character arc is undercooked Kiara Advani is completely wasted and her presence reduced to glamour without depth Anil Kapoor Ashutosh Rana K C Shankar Varun Badola do whats asked of them but leave no lasting impact Bobby Deol also makes a fine cameo appearanceUltimately War 2 is a prime example of a film that mistakes scale for substance Watch it only if youre a diehard fan of the leads or want to see the Spy Universe in completion Otherwise this is one mission you can skipDirection 25 Acting 255 Action 255 Story 155 Screenplay 155

LEAVE YOUR BRAINS AT HOME AND THEN COME TO SEE WAR 2 This should be the tagline of this movieas it was extremely illogical in every way especially when it came to the action sequences and immediate recoveries from the worst of fatal injuriesattacksHrithik Roshan seems to be the Hindi version of the indestructible RajniKant who just couldnt be defeatedunbeaten under any possible circumstances Totally senseless and absolutely illogical action which even for a die hard fan is way beyond belief As is the norm with 98 of the Hindi movies being made for the last 5 years or sothe script was not given any priority and the focus was purely on the spectacular action sequences executed by HrithikNT Rama Rao All in alla complete action packed film based on the 2 major stars and zero script This 170 minute movie except for the outstanding locales was COMPLETEABSOLUTE AND TOTAL GARBAGE010

War 1 was renowned for its tight and gripping screenplay but War 2 fell short in this regard As a viewer I found myself bored throughout most of the movies runtime While there were a few actionpacked scenes they lacked the intensity of letting the main protagonist take a risk and lose the fight Without this element of uncertainty the fight scenes became predictable and lost their appeal For instance one of the leading heroes performed a stunt that was practically impossible making it clear that it was CGIrendered which reduced the movies believabilityAnother issue I had with the movie was its prerelease marketing The focus was solely on the bikini scene of Kiara Advani which seemed completely unrelated to the script and felt forced in its inclusion for marketing purposes This marketing strategy exposed the bias against female actors who despite playing the roles of decorated military personnel were portrayed as mere seductive pool swimmersIn conclusion I would recommend waiting for the movies OTT release instead of watching it in theaters

I just got back from seeing it Overall I like itIll go over what I like first Hrithik Roshan is cool likeable credible and charismatic I couldnt picture another actor in his role I also like the supporting cast members like Anil Kapoor NTR Kiara and others The action scenes are in many cases welldone The best scene is the opening scene involving a samurai sword fight it kinda reminded me of a clip from the John Wick franchise The airplane scene later on is also thrilling Im not usually a fan of the songs in Bollywood movies but I like the one with Hrithik and NTRNo spoilers but the movie uses some flashbacks to flesh out Hrithik and NTRs characters The young actors who play the younger version of their characters look the part The casting was well done hereThe movie has the following issues though I like the action scenes most of the time I didnt like the music blaring in the background The slow motion works most of the time but on a few occasions the effect is cheesy Some of the characters perform feats that push the movie into Marvel DC comic book territory its as if Hrithik and NTR both had doses of supersoldier serum Some parts are almost comedy In the finale we get a fight scene that occurs in a place that looks like Supermans fortress In another scene Hrithik and one of the supporting cast members are getting shot at by many henchman in a parking lot yet theyre able to stand behind a pillar and talk like theyre in a cafeThe romance between Hrithik and Kiara wasnt right They make clear that her father was like a father to Hrithik since Hrithik was a boy I feel that their relationship shouldve been more like siblings than husbandwife Its as if the film just felt that they had to insert a romance subplot somewhere so they jammed this oneStill I really enjoyed this one Im glad I went to the theaters to see it I think its better than the first War movie8510

War 2 attempts to build upon the actionpacked legacy of its predecessor but it ultimately falls short coming across as the weakest film in the franchise While the movie is a feast for the eyes and focuses heavily on cinematic grandeur it struggles significantly with its narrative losing its grip on the plot and lacking an engaging screenplayThe films visual spectacle is its primary focus The action sequences are undoubtedly impressive in scale and the production design is lavish However this dedication to grandeur seems to come at the expense of a welldeveloped and compelling story The plot feels disjointed and the screenplay lacks the emotional depth needed to make the high stakes truly resonate with the audienceThe movies lack of a strong emotional core is particularly noticeable in the background storyThe characters motivations and backstories are not explored with enough depth making it difficult to form a genuine connection with them As a result the film becomes a series of impressive set pieces without a solid foundation to hold them togetherWhile the action may be a good distraction the lack of narrative engagement and emotional depth makes War 2 a less satisfying experience than the first film Its a visually appealing movie but one that leaves you feeling unfulfilled

From the first note of the opening theme you know War 2 means business The score swells and shifts perfectly to match the emotion of each scene adding an extra layer of intensity There are moments where the music builds tension so well that you find yourself gripping the armrest waiting for the explosion of action you know is coming

Its rare these days to find a film that makes you so absorbed you forget to check your phone or wonder how much time is left but War 2 did exactly that for me From the moment the first scene began I was completely drawn into its world The scale is massive but it never sacrifices the intimacy of the characters journeys The action is breathtaking but what impressed me most is how seamlessly it flows into the emotional beats There are several sequences where you can feel your heart pounding not just because of explosions or stunts but because you actually care about the outcome for these people And when the climax hits its so earned that you almost want to stand up and cheer This isnt just an action movie its an experience that stays with you long after the credits roll

If you ever wondered what would happen if an action movie was written entirely by Hritik Roshans mirror this is your answer War 2 isnt a movie its a 2hour shampoo commercial with guns slow motion and an ego the size of a CGI wolfWe open with an old rich man celebrating his 75th birthday outside in the cold because heating is for peasants feeding his digital pet dire wolf The doors to his convertible mansion are of course unlocked because why would you lock your house when Hritik Roshan is the one breaking in The CGI wolf charges at him but then pauses midair as if thinking Wait that hair That beard That jawline Thats the real alpha They share a silent moment of respect before teaming up man and wolf against logic itselfInside the house Hritik struts in slow motion because walking at normal speed is for extras toothpick in his mouth blood on his cheek eyes glowing like a cologne commercial He tells the old man hes here to kill him but only after announcing it dramatically Because villains deserve closure tooThen comes the sword fight Four men with samurai blades charge him Hritik grabs his guns then drops them because apparently guns are for cowards Sure that makes sense We move on He proceeds to fight the four guys in a beautifully choreographed sequence where every hit defies gravity physics and basic anatomy One sword literally falls from the sky and he catches it midair Where did it come from Heaven probably because even Gods a Hritik fanNext eight new guys show up but politely wait for their turn to die Hritik draws a halfcircle on the floor with his sword for no reason at all Maybe its art maybe its geometry maybe its foreplay Then he slides across the floor killing everyone like hes auditioning for Disney on Ice The Blood EditionInside about thirtyfive bad guys are chilling until someone yells Action and they all jump through paper walls like confused origami Not one of them uses the perfectly fine doors Why would they Doors are for mortals Hritik punches kicks flexes smirks and by the time the rap music kicks in all thirtyfive are suddenly gone Vanished Perhaps they too realized they were in War 2 and decided to quit midsceneThe grand finale is pure cinematic poetry The 75yearold villain flees to a helicopter guarded by two gunwielding women They unload hundreds of bullets but not one dares to touch Hritik The bullets terrified of ruining his perfect skin take a sharp Uturn midair He jumps slices a bridge in half dont ask why catches a gun in midair dropped by one of the women and wishes the old man a happy birthday before blowing him to bits Touching ReallyIve seen video game cutscenes with more realism and better storytelling The only thing this movie kills is your brain cellsBy the end War 2 isnt a film its a fever dream designed by a gym mirror and edited by Red Bull But if your idea of cinema is watching Hritik Roshan defy gravity mortality and storytelling well congratulations This is your Citizen Kane

Just came out from the First show of War 2 in the US and was overall entertained It is a good action packed movie but does go a bit over the top for me The highlights of the movie are its very well executed action set pieces with good stunt work and VFX The story arcs for the characters of Colonel Luthra Kabir and Vikram are well written NTR Jrs role has some pretty interesting twists and turns while Kiara not only looks stunning but also packs a real punch The couple of new characters introduced into the Spy Universe are also pretty Jhakaas and AlphaThe main attraction of the movie is of course Hrithik He looks gritty real his age and absolutely fantastic which is probably the reason my wife loved the movie His high octane entry scene his thrilling action sequences his stylish dance moves his trademark trembling face of anger and his teary eyed monologue are all present in the movie Hrithik carries the movie but NTR Jrs fans will not be disappointed when he makes his entry delivers some chill action sequences shakes a fancy leg reveals an interesting origin story and has an over the top but cool finaleThe movie is long but is pretty quick moving from one action packed set piece to another However in order to enjoy the movie one has to be fine with Kabirs Alpha wolf dominates a real fierce wolf just with his sheer presence and looks takes on an army of Katana wielding Ninjas drives a car in the most unexpected places performs some mind boggling stunts on boats planes and trains dodges and even survives some really lethal bullets The songs are good but not at the same level as the original war The midcredit scene introducing the next chapter of the Spy Universe is pretty nice especially the unexpected cameo The mention of Pathaan and Tiger is cool but underwhelming because of their complete absence from the movieOn the YRF Spy Universe scale War 2 lands somewhere between War and Pathaan It doesnt rise up to the level of Ek Tha Tiger and Tiger Zinda Hai but also doesnt take a dive to the Tiger 3 level Its a fun entertaining watch for the action sequences Hrithik and Spy Universe fans but dont expect a lot of heart and goosebumps 7510

Synopsis Rogue agent Kabir Hrithik is believed to have turned traitor but hes secretly working undercover to dismantle a global crime syndicate call Kali cartel When his former ally Vikram Jr NTR is sent to hunt him down their clash sets off a highstakes battle of loyalty deception and redemption as both men confront hidden truths that could decide the fate of the nation1 Explosion Sequences like katana fight Ocean chase Snow mountain showdown are visually stunning Some shots feel similar to Mission Impossible Fast the furious films But others like NTR opening Plane rescue and Train chase suffer from lack of realism or unpolished CGI that doesnt always hold up Fells like watching superhero film instead of grounded reallife spy film2 Story Timing The nearly 3hrs runtime stretches patience It takes 1hr to get into the main plot Too many actions in so little time leave no room for character growth or emotion Some moments drag making twists predictable and the main threat vague A few scenes couldve been tighter and more impactful3 Star Power Hrithik shines as Kabir balancing action and emotion NTR brings energy in fights but his role feels underwritten Kiara Anil are limited and underused though Kiaras sexy bikini look was worth it adds charm Also Emotional flashbacks like KabirRaghu friendship add some depthWhile speaking about the song it doesnt mach even close to jai jai shiv or nattu nattu Whoever direct this doesnt knowy anything about dance songs4 VFX Compared to the first film visuals havent improved much despite 5 years gap The first still outperforms in execution though the sequel offers bigger scale richer colors and exotic locales5 Suggestions For best experience use AMOLED or Full HD display or enable mx players new smart enhance mode6 Overall 68 For fans of big visual fights it impresses for 12 watches But its cohesion narrative and screenplay hold it back from blockbuster true status Those preferring tighter plots or character arcs may find it lacking

The latest installment in the YRF Spy Universe helmed by Ayan Mukerji War 2 banks heavily on the star power and charisma of its lead actors Hrithik Roshan and Jr NTR while neglecting the substance that could power the story and build an overall narrative Right from the very first scene the movies approach is clearly defined and remains consistent throughout its 180minute runtime While the film has its moments they are too few to truly elevate the experience The reallife cinematography with foregn locations is quite good but the moment heavy VFX kicks in the quality drops substantially Songs are decent though JanaabEAli lacked the punch The background score blasts at full force throughout the filmHrithik Roshan looks fabulous performs his action sequences quite well Jr NTRs character was quite lively multifaceted a welcome departure from previous YSU villains Kiara Advani plays her part well though her onscreen chemistry with Hrithik doesnt quite click Ashutosh Rana however is undoubtedly the highlight delivering multiple noteworthy scenes and impactful dialogues Action sequences are a mixed bag some work others feel abruptly ended The mid credits scene is enjoyable Multiple plot points were conveniently added then forgotten about and the runtime is too stretched Overall War 2 is a typical action masala entertainer made on a grand budget that is a onetimewatch affairOVERALL 410

War 2 delivers a decent cinematic experience but falls short of creating the same impact as its predecessor The storyline doesnt bring much freshness and feels somewhat similar to the first War However the fight sequences and action choreography are impressive keeping the audience engaged throughoutHrithik Roshan once again steals the show with his powerful screen presence and flawless performance The entire cast has done a commendable job adding depth to the otherwise predictable plot While the VFX and background settings are good the movie somehow loses its grip due to inconsistent directionOverall War 2 is an average action entertainer worth watching for the stunning fight scenes and Hrithik Roshans brilliance but dont expect a groundbreaking story

In War 2 you get the sense that every action has a consequence When a building blows up its not just a spectacle you see the fallout the impact it has on the characters and the mission This attention to consequence makes the film feel more grounded and raises the tension because you know that the characters arent invincible That unpredictability keeps you engaged right up to the final scene

The films biggest asset is the combined star power of Hrithik Roshan and Jr NTR Their onscreen chemistry and intense rivalry are the films main draw Hrithik brings his signature swagger and charisma to the role of Kabir while Jr NTR matches him with a brooding and powerful presence Their faceoff both physically and emotionally is the central pillar of the film and will surely satisfy fans of both actors

I had quite high expectations from this movie going by what the director has directed in the past Think of Brahmastra wake up Sid Yeh Jawani Hai Deewani etc But the tough lesson learned with this movie was that no matter how good at Director when they work for Yash Raj productions There forced to turn into puppets merely puppets Even if that means the movie goes bonkers it seems the top guys at Yashraj fiddle too much with the production values story scripting overall production leaving a Director with no choice but to follow suit In my observation I have seen it over and over only with the Raj films productions Because guess what the same director did great movies when he worked with Dharma productions for example This movie War 2 has such terrible horrifying logic defying action sequences that you want to bury your head in the sand Take the action in the air on top of a plane with the two main male characters fighting without any wires slipping from the front to the back vice versa but nobody falls off And so many other logic gravity defying nonsense action sequences The only saving grace of the movie is The action sequences in Spain maybe But besides that its a drag of a movie That just goes on and on boring you wanting you to wait for the movie to end any time soon but it does not It goes on and on and bore you to Boredom I have decided Im not going to watch any Yashraj films moving forward period

War 2 2025 Movie Review The YRF Spy Universe was never known for James Bondlevel sophistication or the gritty realistic charm of a film like Baby but it had at one point become a massive moneyspinner That momentum however seems to have died after Pathaan Tiger 3 dented the franchises credibility and now War 2 drags it to a new low This sequel stands out not just as one of the weakest entries in the Spy Universe but also as one of the most underwhelming followups in recent Bollywood memory Hrithik Roshan and Jr NTR team up here but instead of fireworks what we get is a chaotic mess that quickly turns into a patience test Ayan Mukerjis handling of the material is shockingly poor stretching the runtime into something nearly unbearableThe plot centers around RAW agent Kabir Hrithik Roshan who appears to have gone rogue but is in reality still working undercover for the country A new threat emerges in the form of the mysterious antagonist Kali forcing Kabir to infiltrate dangerous territory To do so he has to work against his mentor Colonel Luthra Ashutosh Rana thereby becoming a national enemy in the agencys eyes The RAW forms a new elite team that includes Luthras daughter Kavya Kiara Advani and one of their top yet egodriven agents Vikram aka Raghu Jr NTR Kabir and Raghu share a complicated past making their eventual faceoff more personal and intenseor at least thats what the film wants us to believe Unfortunately the execution falls far shortThe problems begin with the writing Aditya Chopras story is a jumble of halfbaked ideas while Shridhar Raghavans screenplay unfolds like a patchy TV soapepisodic repetitive and often cringeworthy Plot holes pop up every 10 minutes The flashback to Kabir and Raghus childhood is a prime example of misguided storytelling derailing whatever narrative momentum the film had The casting of Kabir as a browny teenager who magically transforms into a Greekgodlevel handsome man is unintentionally hilarious Worse still is the absurd idea of an army officer recruiting Indias best future soldiers from a juvenile detention center for underage criminals Who approved this Logic clearly took an extended vacation during the scripting processThe KabirRaghu rivalry never progresses their confrontations repeatedly reset to square one killing any sense of escalation Kavyas character is practically a decorative piece and the threat posed by Kali is underdeveloped to the point of irrelevance The film also raises absurd questionshow is it so easy to join RAW turn rogue and then conveniently assemble your own team Kabir broke up with Kavya because of his job but he romanced Naina without any fear how The franchise had already stretched believability in Ek Tha Tiger and Pathaan with a RAW agent with an ISI agent but War 2 disregards basic narrative coherence altogetherOn the performance front Hrithik Roshan still looks like a million bucks His swagger is intacteven a casual walk oozes stylebut his character lacks the gritty edge promised in the promos Colonel Luthras claim about Kabir becoming extra violent is laughable given the almost sanitized action on display Jr NTR is a miscast from the word go His accent and overthetop attitude quickly become grating and instead of easing up they get more irritating as the film progresses Kiara Advanis role is tiny and underwritten adding nothing substantial to the plot Anil Kapoor gets a supposedly highoctane gunblazing sequence but its staged so embarrassingly that its hard to watch Ashutosh Rana is serviceable in his cameo while the rest of the cast barely registersTechnically War 2 is a mixed bag The color grading is overly glossy and while the cinematography is passable it offers some outdated lifeless visuals Editing is a serious flawthe film becomes sluggish soon after Hrithiks introductory sequence with the second half and especially the final quarter being outright punishing The production design is competent but the VFX work is shockingly poor especially for a film of this scaleAyan Mukerji whose strengths lie in romcoms and sliceoflife dramas seems out of his club here His approach to action and scifi was already shaky in Brahmastra but that film at least had commendable VFX to mask its issues Here with no such saving grace the flaws are magnified Its baffling how a director entrusted with backtoback Rs 400 crore projects could deliver such backtoback disappointments War 2 is more than just a stumble for the Spy Universeits a fullblown collapse that will require a massive creative overhaul to recover fromIn the end War 2 is a wasteof money time and talent What was marketed as a highoctane faceoff between two megastars turns into one of the most tedious logicdefying and poorly crafted bigbudget films in recent years For a franchise that once promised adrenalinepumping thrills this is the equivalent of serving a gourmet meal with stale leftoversRATING 310

The writing in War 2 deserves praise for giving us lines that linger long after the credits roll Whether its a sharp exchange between rivals or a heartfelt confession the dialogue feels authentic and memorable Its the kind of script where you can imagine fans quoting their favorite moments for years to come Some of the shots in this film could be framed and hung on a wall The way light color and movement are used makes War 2 visually stunning Even during highspeed chases or chaotic battles the visuals are clear and intentional never losing that cinematic beauty

Years after going rogue former intelligence agent Kabir Dhaliwal has become the nations most wanted man To stop him the government deploys its best Special Units Officer Vikram A master tactician with a complex past Vikram is the only operative with the resolve and skill to neutralize his dangerous and elusive targetThe three biggest pillars of any action film are VFX action and story and on all three fronts War 2 is extremely poor For the biggest film of the Spy verse where budget was no constraint having such terrible graphics is downright shameful For most parts the VFX of War 2 cant even compete with PlayStation 3 video games Three of the films major action set pieces one on top of a train another involving an airplane and bomber jet and one Boat action set in dubai are so poorly executed that audiences will laugh at the action and fume at the VFXThe story is the same old recycled plot that the audience will easily predict halfway through Interval Block when the main twist is revealed is not shocking at all Some scenes and twists would have been better left out entirely The film is flat slow and completely devoid of clapworthy moments and standout elevation scenes which are a must for a bigbudget action spectacleJr NTRs fans will likely be very disappointed as he did not get the kind of elevation he deserved His character Raghu has been written far more weakly compared to Hrithiks The entry sequence that should have been his highlight is a complete misfire and in the second half he is totally wasted Though he acted very well his dialogue delivery and hindi is on pointAyan Mukerjis direction is poor but when the story and visual effects themselves are so bad theres little a director can do There is no proper justification for any character in the film including the leads Their missions and methods follow are illogical and both the emotion and the conflict between Hrithik and NTR completely fail to landIn HRNTR childhood scenes Mumbai shows todays 5060 floor skyscrapers probably 30 years ago Even Hrithiks younger version has wheatish skin black eyes while grownup Kabir has green eyes and fair skin With such basic detailing errors what can you expect from the storyThe twists and turns also fail miserably The movie remains boring slow and without any high points Hrithik Roshan as Kabir looks outstanding but even he cannot rise above the script His entry and the entire Japan action sequence are choreographed very poorly The films writer Sridhar Raghavan sank Tiger 3 and has now damaged the future of the Spy Universe with War 2 Can you believe it The organisation aiming to destroy India in War 2 is called KALI pronounced Kali not Kaali but still an avoidable name that could spark controversy More surprising is that in todays time a film of War 2s scale ends up with such a weak villain group and flimsy arcKiara Advani is only for glamour and wearing neon green bikini and Anil Kapoor is totally wasted Only Major Highlight of the film is Jaanabe Ali Song and Dance of both the leads Overall War 2 fails on every front It stands nowhere in comparison to Pathaan War or Ek ThaTiger and is even weaker than Tiger3 The film has neither style nor substance

If youve ever wondered what happens when you mix stale plotlines borrowed stunts and music that makes you question your own taste in sound War 2 is here to answer and disappoint in spectacular styleHrithik Roshan appears like hes been asked to film this between power naps His expressions dont just say Im tired they whisper Ive read the script and my enthusiasm has left the building The central threat this time A Syndicate plotting to destabilise Indiaa concept so outdated it could have been a bonus chapter in a 1986 spy novelAnd then comes the big reveal our evershifting protagonist and antagonist dont try to keep track they swap roles so often youd need a flowchart are actually former street kids from Bombay footpaths After petty thefts both end up in a juvenile home and in this cinematic universe RAWs recruitment process apparently extends to remand homes because nothing says national security like picking agents with a history of bread theft One of the two gets selected for his patriotic delivery of deshbhakti dialog the other doesnt inspite of better performance in a 50m steeple chase race and thus a lifelong rivalry beginsBut the twists keep coming the antagonist is actually the protagonist the protagonist is branded a villain the real antagonist becomes a double agent inside RAW while plotting against India and somehow lands in the PMs security detail at Davos where everything right upto PMs aircraft is compromised Fit in between creatively picturized chases between two speed boats that jump from the sea around Abu Dhabi flying over Ferrari world and land on the F1 track where a F1 car race is actually happening The boats successfully dodge the speeding cars from opposite direction and land back into the seaAnother mid air action scene sees both of them successfully destroying a B2 bomberIn yet another original move the mastermind funding the syndicates turns out to be a minister who is a wannabe prime minister Because nothing freshens a wellworn spy plot like adding the corrupt politician tropeThe socalled pice de rsistance An action sequence in Italy so blatantly lifted from Mission Impossible Dead Reckoning you halfexpect Tom Cruise to stroll in and ask for royaltiesJunior NTR is the only saving grace his energy feels like it came from a far better Telugu film and got trapped here by accidentThe rest is an exhausting mix of overthetop set pieces awkward execution childish CGI and background music so intrusive it could qualify as a public health risk If Bollywood wanted to raise a were completely of ideas flag War 2 just unfurled it in IMAXPro tip Bring a strip of Disprin tablets not for the headache but for the regret youll feel afterwards for having ignored this reviewIn the end of this never ending trauma NTR returns to Tollywood and Roshan returns to familiar territory home because at this point only Krrish 4 might restore his balanceThe next chapter is introduced with Bobby Deol who by the way still has a choice to walk out and save his resurgent career

War 2 was supposed to be one of the biggest cinematic events of the year With Hrithik Roshan returning as Kabir a proven action superstar and with Ayan Mukerji at the helm under the Yash Raj banner expectations were sky high Sadly the film doesnt come anywhere close to living up to its hype Its a frustrating case of a talented actor carrying a film that is otherwise let down by poor direction sloppy writing uneven pacing and some shockingly mediocre VFX for a production of this scaleLets start with what works Hrithik Roshan Once again he proves why he is a true panIndian superstar His screen presence is magnetic his charisma effortless and he performs action and emotional beats with the same conviction Even in scenes where the dialogue is weak or the staging feels uninspired Hrithik manages to elevate the moment Watching him on screen reminds you why the original War worked in the first placehe embodies the suave conflicted largerthanlife hero archetype so well that you want the film to be worthy of his performance Unfortunately the film fails him at every turnThe biggest problem lies in Ayan Mukerjis direction While Ayan has shown promise with films like Yeh Jawaani Hai Deewani and attempted worldbuilding in Brahmastra War 2 needed sharp taut storytelling and clear vision What we get instead is a confused mess that tries to juggle too many things at once The film meanders with unnecessary subplots halfbaked character arcs and emotional beats that dont land The pacing is erraticlong stretches of dull exposition are suddenly interrupted by overthetop action sequences that feel disconnected from the narrative Instead of creating a tight spy thriller Ayan turns it into a patchwork of mismatched ideasThe writing is equally disappointing Dialogues lack punch the story feels recycled and plot twists are painfully predictable For a franchise that is supposed to be Indias answer to global action blockbusters War 2 feels strangely dated in its storytelling Characters make illogical decisions just to move the plot forward and the supposed emotional depth comes across as hollow because the script doesnt invest in relationships or stakes By the end you feel no real tension no surprise no reason to care about who lives or diesAnd then theres the technical side where Yash Raj Films truly drops the ball The VFX and CGI are shockingly poor for a film of this scale and budget Explosions look fake green screen shots are glaringly obvious and several sequencesmeant to be jawdroppingend up unintentionally laughable Instead of immersing you in the action the visuals constantly remind you of how artificial everything looks For a studio that wants to compete with Hollywood this level of execution is simply unacceptableCinematography and editing dont fare much better While a few action set pieces have moments of flair most sequences are either shot too dark too chaotic or cut so rapidly that you cant follow the choreography The lack of coherence makes it impossible to enjoy what should have been War 2s strongest asset its actionThe music and background score are forgettable failing to add tension or energy to key scenes In a franchise where songs like Ghungroo became cultural moments War 2 doesnt offer a single memorable track Instead of enhancing the story the soundtrack feels like an afterthoughtUltimately War 2 is a disappointment because it had all the right ingredients to be great a charismatic star in Hrithik Roshan a successful franchise foundation and the backing of one of Indias biggest production houses But bad direction lazy writing and poor technical execution sink the film Hrithik deserved better and audiences deserved betterThis isnt the thrilling edgeoftheseat spectacle fans were promised Its a wasted opportunity

Some of the wide shots in War 2 are so beautifully composed that I wished I could pause the screen just to take them in The scale of the production is jawdropping from sprawling landscapes to intricately designed sets that feel livedin Its not just the big scenes that impress though even the small intimate moments are visually striking Its rare to see this level of attention to visual detail in an action blockbuster and it makes the whole experience feel grand and memorable

Hrithik Roshan starrer War 2 is unfortunately not as good as its predecessor Yes you will enjoy the electrifying onscreen presence of Hrithik Roshan alongside NTR but the storyline fails to truly engage For those who love nonstop action the movie delivers plenty of highoctane sequences and fans of Hrithik and NTR will find moments to cheer forHowever because of how impactful the last installment was I as an audience member expected much more this time Sadly the film does not live up to that benchmark In theatres people shouted and clapped during certain scenes but the lack of a strong gripping narrative meant those moments didnt leave a lasting impressionThe songs are entirely skippable adding little value to the overall experience While the chemistry between the leads and the wellchoreographed fight scenes provide some entertainment they cant mask the weak storytellingPersonally I would call War 2 a onetime watch Youll hear from others that its awesome or mindblowing but I would advise not to go in with high expectations Enjoy it for the star power and action but dont expect the same magic as the first WarWar 2 Review by itsbymayank Mayank Bharat Bhushan Louteriya

Storyline Collapse Unlike War 1s tight and engaging plot War 2 is riddled with illogical events and lazy writing making it impossible to take seriouslyPhysics Thrown Out the Window Action sequences are so overthetop and gravitydefying that they border on cartoonish rather than thrillingWeak Music Songs are forgettable and feel like forced fillers dragging the pace instead of elevating itMiscast Lead NTR Jr though a skilled actor feels completely out of place in this role due to poor character design and integrationWasted Talent Kiara Advani capable of strong performances is reduced to mere eye candy with no meaningful contribution to the storyDisrespectful Representation The portrayal of the Indian Army and RAW is exaggerated unrealistic and at times disrespectful turning them into props rather than portraying them with dignityLack of Emotional Core The film lacks the emotional depth and character development that made War 1 memorableCashGrab Feel Relies on flashy visuals and loud set pieces instead of a wellcrafted narrative making it feel like an empty sequel made to exploit the brand

Ladies and gentlemen we have witnessed cinematic history War 2 has officially set the record as the biggest disaster in Indian cinema surpassing even the legendary catastrophe that was Thugs of Hindostan This isnt just a movie its a masterclass in how to spectacularly waste INR400 crores and the careers of two supremely talented actors in one fell swoopThe Aditya Chopra School of Storytelling Where Logic Goes to DieLets start with the mastermind behind this debacleAditya Chopra who apparently decided that having a coherent story is overrated The plot is so recycled and predictable that audiences were calling out twists halfway through the movie When your interval block twist isnt shocking at all you know youve failed at the most basic level of storytellingChopras original story feels like it was generated by an AI that only watched mediocre Hollywood spy thrillers from the 2000s The narrative is so weak that even the actors looked confused about their own motivations Its the kind of story that makes you wonder if Chopra actually watched his own previous successes or just assumed audiences would accept any garbage wrapped in expensive packagingAyan Mukerji The Director Who Made Brahmastra Look Like Citizen KaneIf Chopra provided the foundation of mediocrity Ayan built a skyscraper of incompetence on top of it This is the same director who gave us the visual spectacle of Brahmastra yet somehow managed to make War 2s action sequences look like they were choreographed by a caffeinated internMukerjis direction is so poor that even PlayStation 3 video games have better visual coherence The man took two of the biggest superstars from North and South India and made them look like expensive mannequins going through the motions His attempt at creating elevation scenes for both actors fails so miserably that Jr NTRs fans are probably considering a classaction lawsuitThe train sequencewhich should have been the films crown jewelinstead becomes a comedy of errors where NTR is apparently superhuman enough to separate train bogies with his bare hands Its not action its cartoon physics with a INR400 crore budgetThe Great VFX Catastrophe When Money Cant Buy QualityThe VFX is so atrociously bad that it makes the audience laugh during what should be intense action sequences For a film with an unlimited budget having graphics that cant compete with decadeold video games is not just disappointingits shamefulThe three major action set piecesthe train sequence the airplane and bomber jet scene and the Dubai boat chaseare executed so poorly that they break any immersion the audience might have had These arent just technical failures theyre insults to the intelligence of moviegoers who paid premium prices to watch this digital disasterWasted Talent How to Ruin Two Superstars in Under Three HoursHrithik Roshan the Greek God of Bollywood deserved so much better than this script Despite his magnetic screen presence and effortless action sequences hes completely let down by the uninspired writing His Kabir feels less sharp and intelligent compared to the original War reduced to a glorified action figure spouting generic dialogueJr NTR making his Hindi debut gets perhaps the worst treatment of all His character arc is so undercooked that it borders on criminal negligence The entry sequence that should have introduced him as a powerhouse performer is instead a complete misfire For an actor of his caliberwho gave us the electrifying performances in RRRto be reduced to this is nothing short of tragicThe film wastes Jr NTR so completely that his fans are justified in feeling cheated The man who could effortlessly match Hrithik in dance sequences is given such poorly choreographed material that their faceoff looks more like a corporate teambuilding exercise than a clash of titansYRFs Spy Universe The Cinematic Equivalent of the TitanicThe socalled YRF Spy Universe has officially jumped the shark with War 2 What started as a promising franchise with Ek Tha Tiger has devolved into a formulaic soulless moneygrabbing exercise The universe lacks any cohesive vision relying instead on random cameos and crossreferences that feel more like desperate fanservice than organic storytellingThe Final Verdict A INR400 Crore Lesson in How Not to Make MoviesWar 2 isnt just badits catastrophically historically legendarily terrible Its a film so devoid of substance that it makes Tiger 3 look like a masterpiece in comparison The movie fails on every conceivable level story direction VFX character development and basic entertainment valueThis is what happens when you prioritize scale over substance when you assume star power can compensate for creative bankruptcy War 2 will be remembered not as an action thriller but as a cautionary tale about the dangers of corporate filmmaking run amokThe real tragedy isnt just the INR150 crore loss at the box officeits the criminal waste of talent time and audience goodwill In trying to create their Avengers YRF instead created their Justice League except somehow even worseSkip this cinematic disaster and save yourself three hours of your life Even diehard fans of Hrithik and Jr NTR deserve better than this expensive insult to intelligence

War 2 ReviewWar 2 delivers on scale and spectacle but struggles to hold ground when it comes to storytellingWhat Works The films biggest strength lies in its breathtaking action sequences executed with slick choreography and grand visuals that keep you hooked Hrithik Roshan mesmerizes in every frame effortlessly commanding attention with his presence and performance Kiara Advani despite a limited role impresses in key moments that carry weight Jr NTR brings in a refreshing energy with his fun and natural dialogue delivery adding a dose of charm to the narrativeWhat Doesnt Work The excitement often gets overshadowed by a predictable and weak storyline that fails to surprise The script leans heavily on crowdpleasing dialogues that feel forced rather than organic An unnecessary dance number and an outofplace bikini sequence add little value dragging the flow of the film At nearly three hours the runtime feels stretchedtighter editing could have made the experience far more gripping And unlike the first installment where the music gave us some memorable tracks War 2 offers a soundtrack that is a major letdown and adds no recall value to the filmVerdict War 2 is a visual spectacle powered by its stars and action but if you go in expecting a strong story memorable music or tight pacing you may walk out underwhelmed Worth a watch for Hrithiks magnetic presence and the largerthanlife action but dont expect much beyond that

Ive never experienced anything as maddening as the editing style in many Bollywood films today Every half a second feels like a completely new take resulting in a jarring and exhausting viewing experience Its as if the filmmakers believe fast cuts and rapid scene changes can mask poor acting or weak storytelling Instead it just makes it impossible to connect with the characters or get immersed in the storyThe relentless barrage of quick cuts doesnt build tension or excitement it shatters any sense of flow and coherence Watching such heavily chopped films leaves me more confused than entertained If this is supposed to be the signature style it needs a serious rethink Please bring back scenes that breathe moments that feel real and editing that respects the viewers attention spanEditing should enhance a movie not turn it into a frantic disjointed mess Until that happens I will avoid Bollywood films that suffer from this editing nightmare

War 2 promised to raise the bar for Indian action cinema but what it delivered was a spectacularly empty logic defying exercise in style over substance Despite its mega budget starstudded cast and global locations the film is a pathetic viewing experience proving that a blockbuster budget cannot compensate for a bankrupt scriptThe films most glaring failure lies in its action sequences which are as far from reality as it gets While high octane stunts are a staple of the genre War 2 takes the suspension of disbelief and throws it out of a well a flying airplane The much hyped climactic fight scene over a flying airplane is the most ludicrous example featuring two protagonists defying gravity momentum and all laws of physics in a sequence that feels more like an animated cartoon than a liveaction thriller The sheer absurdity of the choreography makes it impossible to engage with the tension It simply induces eyerolls and headshakesBeyond the physically impossible action the story has absolutely no gripThe plot is a confusing convoluted mess of doublecrosses and international espionage that relies on cheap clichs and unearned emotional beats Characters are thinly written serving only as vehicles for the next extravagant set piece The central conflict which attempts to be a high stakes face off feels cosmetic and hollow because the narrative never grounds its stakes or develops a compelling reason for the audience to care about the protagonists cat and mouse gameIn essence Its a prime example of a film that mistakes spectacle for cinema Save your money and your brain cells this war is a decisive loss

Ive seen a lot of action blockbusters that were visually spectacular but left me feeling empty afterward War 2 is the complete opposite Sure the visuals are topnotch and the stunt work rivals anything coming out of Hollywood but the story here has genuine weight Every big set piece feels connected to the narrative not just thrown in for the sake of spectacle I loved how the characters werent onedimensional even in the quietest moments you learn something new about them The editing is sharp the cinematography is gorgeous and the pacing never drags despite the long runtime When I left the theater I felt like Id been taken on a real journey rather than just being shown a collection of cool shots

War 2 fires on all cylinders when it comes to action delivering a highoctane spectacle that keeps your adrenaline surging from the first frame to the last With sharp direction and impressive visual effects every combat sequence feels grand crisp and immersiveThe soundtrack complements the intensity perfectly the BGM is electrifying amplifying the thrill of every scene Amidst all the chaos the film also offers moments of charm with a few meaningful feelgood songs that blend smoothly into the narrative The duo dance in JanaabeAli stands out as a treat for the audienceIf theres one area that leaves you wanting more its the depth of storytelling the plot never digs as deep as it could relying instead on the spectacle of action to drive the momentum But as a pure action entertainer War 2 delivers exactly what it promises edgeofyourseat funAnd heres a tip Stay back for the two postcredit scenes They might just hint at bigger battles ahead

It is a film that will leave you entertained but not in the way the filmmakers intended Its a cinematic spectacle of unintentional humor a thrilling spy saga that accidentally becomes a comedy of errors If you go in expecting a serious action thriller youll be disappointed But if youre looking for a good laugh and a break from the seriousness of the world then War 2 is the perfect ticket Just be prepared to have your expectations subverted in the most amusing way possible

By the time War 2 ended I felt like I had been on the mission myself The film is relentless but not in a way that becomes exhausting Instead it pulls you deeper and deeper into its world with each scene You start in relatively controlled situations but as the stakes rise so does the intensity of the action The handtohand combat scenes are some of the most visceral Ive seen with every blow feeling like it matters The car chases are exhilarating without becoming cartoonish and the shootouts are staged in ways that keep you guessing When you finally get to the end it feels like youve earned the resolution right alongside the characters

terrible graphics is downright shameful For most parts the VFX of War 2 cant even compete with PlayStation 3 video games Three of the films major action set pieces one on top of a train another involving an airplane and bomber jet and one Boat action set in dubai are so poorly executed that audiences will laugh at the action and fume at the VFXThe story is the same old recycled plot that the audience will easily predict halfway through Interval Block when the main twist is revealed is not shocking at all Some scenes and twists would have been better left out entirely The film is flat slow and completely devoid of clapworthy moments and standout elevation scenes which are a must for a bigbudget action spectacleIndian Spy Movies in a nutshell Starts with a destruction of Indian asset and antagonist challenging to catch himSpy agency minister meeting with a photo on big screen and few mapsAntagonist being defined as very dangerous and ex service man or spy dissapointed by some past treatmentNow Only one man can catch him Comes a hero with grand entry dialogues doing some stuff in som3 corner of the worldA female spy joins him for a missionBeach songs danceMission starts Flying objects actors running on bus train jumping off from helicopter planePredictable but marketed as surprised cameosShuru tune Kiya Khatam Me karungaJai HindHrithik character arc is all over the place He hide his identity like he was on some kind of big mission but revealed his identity before he achieved anything I can overlook logics and everything during action scenes but not in storylineAs usual villain characters are jokersIn the movie we have a scene that there are stealth bombers Hrithiks character is flying but that plane is only available in USA and that very select few and highly swims and shows off her hot bodyand in the audience get horny watching her girls and boys included

I just got back from seeing it Overall I like itIll go over what I like first Hrithik Roshan is cool likeable credible and charismatic I couldnt picture another actor in his role I also like the supporting cast members like Anil Kapoor NTR Kiara and others The action scenes are in many cases welldone The best scene is the opening scene involving a samurai sword fight it kinda reminded me of a clip from the John Wick franchise The airplane scene later on is also thrilling Im not usually a fan of the songs in Bollywood movies but I like the one with Hrithik and NTRNo spoilers but the movie uses some flashbacks to flesh out Hrithik and NTRs characters The young actors who play the younger version of their characters look the part The casting was well done hereThe movie has the following issues though I like the action scenes most of the time I didnt like the music blaring in the background The slow motion works most of the time but on a few occasions the effect is cheesy Some of the characters perform feats that push the movie into Marvel DC comic book territory its as if Hrithik and NTR both had doses of supersoldier serum Some parts are almost comedy In the finale we get a fight scene that occurs in a place that looks like Supermans fortress In another scene Hrithik and one of the supporting cast members are getting shot at by many henchman in a parking lot yet theyre able to stand behind a pillar and talk like theyre in a cafeThe romance between Hrithik and Kiara wasnt right They make clear that her father was like a father to Hrithik since Hrithik was a boy I feel that their relationship shouldve been more like siblings than husbandwife Its as if the film just felt that they had to insert a romance subplot somewhere so they jammed this oneStill I really enjoyed this one Im glad I went to the theaters to see it I think its better than the first War movie

War 2 ReviewI just returned from watching War 2 and I have to say I enjoyed it overallThe positives Hrithik Roshan absolutely carries the film Hes stylish charismatic believable and just plain fun to watchI honestly cant imagine anyone else in his role The supporting cast also shines with Anil Kapoor NTR Kiara Advani and others adding a lot of energy Many of the action sequences are crafted really well The opening samurai sword fight is easily the highlight it gave me vibes of a John Wick scene The airplane sequence later on is another standout packed with tension and spectacleI also have to admit I liked one of the songsespecially the Hrithik and NTR trackeven though Im usually not a fan of Bollywood musical numbers The flashbacks worked nicely too helping flesh out the backstories for Hrithik and NTRs characters The young actors playing their younger selves were spoton in castingThe drawbacks As much as I enjoyed the action I wasnt a fan of the overbearing background music The slowmotion shots generally looked good but a few came off a bit cheesy At times the characters stunts felt like they belonged in a Marvel or DC movie as if Hrithik and NTR had taken supersoldier serum A few scenes bordered on unintentionally funnyfor example the finale fight takes place in a location resembling Supermans fortress and theres a shootout in a parking lot where Hrithik and a teammate casually chat behind a single pillar while dozens of henchmen fire at themThe weakest part for me was the romance subplot between Hrithik and Kiara The film clearly establishes that her father was like a father figure to Hrithik since childhood so their relationship made more sense as brothersister than husbandwife It felt forced as if the filmmakers simply wanted to insert romance somewhere and picked the easiest optionFinal thoughts Despite its flaws War 2 is a very entertaining watch and Im glad I saw it in theaters In my opinion its actually stronger than the first War movieRating 1010

War 2 I rarely write reviews but after watching what I can only describe as the worst movie ever made I felt compelled to warn others This film is a complete disaster from start to finish a total time waste with zero logic no meaningful storyline and action sequences that are more laughable than thrillingFrom the very beginning the movie struggles to find its footing The plot if you can even call it that is nothing more than a string of disconnected scenes stitched together without purpose Characters appear and disappear without explanation their motivations unclear their dialogue forced and shallow The socalled twists are painfully predictable and instead of being surprised I found myself rolling my eyes at how ridiculous everything feltThe action which seems to be the films main selling point is equally unbearable It is loud repetitive and devoid of creativity Watching cars explode and people jump through the air in slow motion for no reason might have been exciting two decades ago but here it just feels like mindless noise There is no tension no thrill only dumb exaggerated stunts that insult the intelligence of the audienceEven worse is the complete lack of emotional connection You dont care about the characters because the film gives you no reason to Their backstories are nonexistent their relationships unbelievable and their decisions make no sense By the end you are simply waiting for the credits to roll so the suffering can finally endOverall this movie is a prime example of how not to make cinema It wastes time money and energy while delivering nothing in return If you value your brain cells your wallet and your sanity stay as far away from this trainwreck as possibleDo you want me to make this more sarcasticfunny like a roast or keep it as a serious negative review

From the very first scene this film had me hooked The action sequences were electrifyingbeautifully choreographed intense and grounded enough to feel real Each fight and chase scene had a unique rhythm that kept the adrenaline flowingBut what really elevated the experience was the suspense The story was layered with twists and turns that kept me guessing until the very end Just when I thought I had it figured out the film would throw in another clever surprise without feeling forcedThe cinematography was a visual treatevery frame captured with precision to enhance both the tension and the spectacle Dark moody shots perfectly complemented the mysterious moments while dynamic camera work amplified the chaos of the actionThe directors control over pacing was exceptional The film knew when to push the intensity and when to let the suspense build quietly in the background This balance made both the thrills and the reveals hit harderPerformances were topnotch The lead actors brought emotional depth making the stakes feel personal and the supporting cast added layers to the mystery You could feel the tension not only in the action but in the characters interactionsThis isnt just an action filmits a suspenseful stylish ride that grips you until the credits roll

Just finished watching what might be the most painfully recycled logicdefying Indian action movie Ive seen in a while and I regret every minuteThe plot Same old Save India from Destruction formula recycled for the hundredth time A rogue exRAW agent because of course it has to be a former agent with a tragic past is the only one who can stop a global threat Why Because the script says so No fresh take no subtlety just a paintbynumbers nationalistic action messThe writing is insultingly weak Dialogues sound like they were lifted from a 2005 rejected soap opera Theres zero emotional weight because every line is either a forced punchline or a lecture on patriotism Not to mention the painfully bad overacting every emotion is dialed up to eleven to the point where it becomes borderline parodyNow lets talk logic or the complete lack thereof Characters teleport from one country to another like theyre using Google Maps shortcuts One minute theyre fighting in Spain next morning theyre back in India and then casually dancing in Spain the next day Yes fighting one day dancing the next Who approved this scriptA fugitive wanted man is somehow traveling in and out of India like hes on a tourist visa No disguise no tension just vibes And dont get me started on the VFX calling it bad would be generous Its shockingly amateurish especially for a movie clearly made with a blockbuster budget CGI explosions fake green screens and slowmo sequences so poorly done it feels like a bad mobile game cutsceneAction scenes Dumb Loud Unrealistic to the point of comedy Theres no creativity or tension just people flying in the air dramatic posing and slow motion punches with laughable sound effectsIf youve seen one of these movies before youve seen this one It brings absolutely nothing new to the table except more illogical sequences louder shouting and worse visual effectsFinal verdict A movie that mistakes noise for excitement overacting for emotion and clichs for storytelling Avoid it unless youre into accidental comedy

I went into War 2 hoping for at least a decent followup to the original What I got instead was two hours of frustration confusion and disappointment Its one of those movies that makes you check the time more than you follow the plot mainly because theres barely a plot to followThe first issue hits almost immediately there is no real story The movie jumps from one action sequence to another without ever explaining why anything is happening Characters appear out of nowhere start fighting and disappear again without reason Theres no emotional buildup no motivation no proper connection between scenes It feels like the script was written overnight by someone who forgot to include dialogue or logicEven the fighting which is supposed to be the movies strong point is painfully bad The choreography makes no sense enemies wait their turn to attack physics vanish and somehow every explosion looks identical At times it feels like watching a video game running on the lowest graphics setting Its supposed to be intense but instead its unintentionally funnyThe visuals are another disaster The graphics and editing look like something from ten years ago Explosions are clearly fake and the CGI backgrounds dont even blend with the characters Lighting changes randomly between shots and you can see hard cuts where scenes were lazily stitched together The camera shakes too much as if theyre trying to hide how little actually happens on screen Calling it cinematic would be generous its just messyActing isnt any better The leads look lost probably because they had no clue what their characters were supposed to do Dialogues sound robotic and most of the time you can tell they were added later in postproduction Theres not a single emotional moment that lands You never feel invested in what happens because the movie gives you no reason to careEven the soundtrack which could have saved a few scenes feels out of place The music either overpowers the dialogue or doesnt match the tone at all A dramatic song plays during random background fights and silent moments drag on with nothing but awkward breathing and footstepsBy the time the movie ends youre left wondering why it was even made War 2 feels like a rushed attempt to cash in on the success of its name It doesnt respect the audiences time attention or intelligence Nothing in it feels original or thoughtout not the fights not the characters and definitely not the storyIf youre looking for action logic or even basic entertainment this isnt the place War 2 is a movie that tries to look big but ends up being hollow from every angle Its not just bad its forgettable Save your time skip this one and watch literally anything else

War 2 is hands down one of the worst films Ive ever endured Its as if the creators assumed the audience is brainless enough to accept anything wrapped in flashy action The casting is off the action is uninspired the story is a mess the direction is clueless and the dialogues are downright cringeworthy Every single element is a disaster I want my time and money back Netflix do us all a favor and remove this train wreck from your platform immediately

Theres no denying that War 2 is technically stunning The cinematography editing and production design are all toptier But what impressed me most is that beneath all the technical perfection theres a beating human heart The story never gets lost in its own spectacle it always comes back to the relationships and choices driving the characters Even in the quiet moments theres a sense of purpose and tension Thats what separates it from other action films that are all style and no substance

There are sequences in War 2 where the characters dont speak a single word yet you know exactly what theyre thinking and feeling Thats because the visuals are doing the heavy lifting from the way a shadow creeps across the frame to the way the camera lingers on a glance or a subtle gesture You get drawn into these unspoken moments just as much as the highoctane set pieces Ive seen plenty of action films that rely entirely on dialogue to push the story forward but here the camera tells its own story often with more impact than the words could Its rare and its beautiful to watch

Asal me ye kya he bhai Purai bakwasWar 2 is a film that mistakes noise and gloss for genuine cinema On paper it promises a stylish spy thriller but what it delivers is a bloated cartoonish spectacle filled with laughably unreal scenesThe story takes a backseat to endless globetrotting sequences that feel stitched together only to showcase exotic backdrops Instead of flowing naturally the plot seems designed around action set pieces leaving the narrative hollow and disjointedThe muchtouted VFX are shockingly poor for a film of this scale The infamous airplane fight scene looks like a lowbudget video game instantly breaking immersion and inviting unintentional laughter Similarly train fights yacht chases and glacier combat sequences are recycled clichs offering nothing new or believableAt nearly three hours long the movie drags painfully Conversations and side plots pile up but none carry weight or emotional depth The film tries to juggle patriotism friendship betrayal and global conspiracies but ends up committing to none of themleaving everything halfbakedWhat hurts most is that with actors of this caliber the film could have been a sharp gripping spy drama Instead it reduces its stars to props in a messy effectsdriven ride that neither thrills nor connectsFor a film with such a massive budget War 2 delivers very little Bloated unreal and ultimately forgettable its one of the weakest entries in YRFs socalled Spy Universe

Just watched War 2 I can see why theres been so much negativity surrounding it Lets discuss a few key pointsFirstly as a sequel to the excellent film War and featuring two of Indian cinemas biggest superstars along with a director who has a 100 success ratio its natural for the publics expectations to be incredibly highUnfortunately many viewers were left disappointed The film often feels like a recycled version of other movies While some individual parts are good the overall screenplay is weak The cinematography with its familiar color palette offers nothing new The visual effects are also a major letdown I wouldnt say the movie is outright bad but its certainly not good it falls somewhere in betweenPositives Hrithik Roshan His presence is simply phenomenal His walk acting dance and all the fight sequences are impressive Hes particularly outstanding in the emotional scenes where his performance is truly a highlightThe Child Actor The young boy who played NTR Jrs childhood role in the flashback scenes delivered a strong performanceNegatives NTR Jrs Performance His acting felt mediocre and onedimensional as he seemed to carry the same facial expression throughout the entire filmWeak VFX The visual effects are noticeably poor The scene where NTR Jr Jumps onto the train and the boat chase sequence are absolute disasters For a film with a budget of 400 crores you have to wonder where the money was spentPredictable Screenplay The story is incredibly predictable and you can easily guess what will happen next This weak execution from Ayan Mukerji was highly unexpectedDisastrous Climax The climax is a complete mess If the writers were determined to keep NTR Jrs character alive they should have found a more creative and satisfying way to end the film instead of delivering such a weak and clumsy conclusionUltimately War 2 is an average movie If youre a Hrithik Roshan fan youll likely enjoy his performance The film could have been much better with a stronger screenplay and a shorter runtime as its excessive length makes it feel boring in some parts

What impressed me most about War 2 is that it doesnt rely solely on its looks even though its visually stunning Every stylish frame is backed up by meaningful story beats and welldeveloped characters The slick cinematography enhances the tension while the emotional undercurrent keeps you grounded You can tell that the filmmakers cared about more than just spectacle they wanted you to feel something And I did I left the theater thinking about certain moments long after the credits rolled

Its rare to find a movie where the intensity of the score is perfectly matched by the performances but War 2 nails it The music is bold cinematic and unforgettable and the actors rise to meet it in every scene Whether its a desperate chase through narrow streets or a quiet conversation in the middle of the night the emotional resonance is the same The lead actor in particular delivers a performance thats full of subtle shifts one moment fierce determination the next quiet vulnerability That kind of range paired with such a commanding score makes this film truly special

War 2 begins with promise setting up the stakes of an espionage thriller but the tension fizzles out soon after What starts like a turbulent plane ride never really smoothens and the spylike threat quickly loses its sting Scenes that could have breathed had they lasted just two seconds longer get drowned out by overthetop CGI explosions Instead of sharp thrills what we get is a typical spy flick taking extreme cinematic liberties where nameless goons fall without the hero breaking a sweatThe screenplay leans heavily on VFX rarely letting character entries or arcs settle in The cast is uniformly strongHrithik Roshan NTR Jr and Kiara Advani all deliverbut the writing and editing undercut them Kiaras best action sequence for instance is chopped up with such haste that it leaves little impactHrithik Roshan remains as magnetic as ever with powerpacked expressions and presence yet his Kabir feels less sharp than before In War 2019 his rogueagent persona had a chilling unpredictability here the film spends more energy branding him as a warrior to echo the title rather than allowing him to be the intelligent agile spy he once was His dynamic with NTR Jr Also never quite gelsmore like oil and water always colliding in CGIheavy action sequences rather than in meaningful onetoone exchangesDirection is the weakest link With a cast of this caliber the film deserved a vision beyond simply following the script Instead Ayan Mukerjis treatment stays safe and middling chasing spectacle over creativity Action set pieces feel like stylish rehashesair water icechecked off a list without much inventivenessThe background score serves more as a tribute to the stars than to the film itself with the original War theme still outshining it Songs are serviceable but forgettable landing like fillers rather than narrative drivers Tonally the movie wavers between serious and overthetop trying to balance emotion with mass action but never making either feel personal or justifiedSum up War 2 had the arsenalstar power scale and franchise goodwillbut misfires by choosing spectacle over soul Hrithik and NTR Jr Are wasted in a film that looks grand but struggles to feel grounded

The film suffers from poor direction The narrative is riddled with illogical sequences Adding to the issues NTRs performance leans heavily into overacting Overall the movie misses the mark both in storytelling and executionThe direction is a masterclass in how not to make a movie despite powerful casting

From the very first faceoff you can sense the underlying tension between the two leads Its not just about fighting its about outsmarting each other Hrithik brings a calm almost calculating energy while NTR is unpredictable and intense That contrast makes every interaction charged with suspense The action scenes are great but its that tension that makes them so satisfying

Right from the opening shot War 2 sets a tone of intensity that never dips for a second Even in its quieter scenes theres this constant sense of anticipation as if something big is always just around the corner This is the kind of pacing that keeps you glued to your seat not because its relentless in a tiring way but because it knows how to balance breathless action with moments of tension You get the best of both worlds sequences that make your pulse race and moments that let you soak in the story without losing the momentum Its a rare balancing act that very few action films get right

War 2 is a disappointing addition to the YRF Spy Universe a film that feels like a forced overstuffed sequel rather than a natural progressionClocking in at nearly three hours the movie dragged on with repetitive action sequences a predictable plot and excessive climax scenes every 10 minutes as if the filmmakers were trying to compensate for a lack of substance with sheer spectacleHrithik Roshans charisma and action prowess are the only saving graces but even his performance cant salvage the weak script and overthetop TeluguTamilstyle theatrics NTR Jr Brings energy but feels misplaced in the YRF universe and Kiara Advanis role is underwritten and forgettable The films attempt to blend RAW espionage with South Indian masala results in a messy tonally inconsistent experienceThe YRF Spy Universe peaked with Tiger Zinda Hai and has been declining ever since War was good but War 2 feels like a cash grab lacking the tight storytelling and emotional depth of its predecessors If this is the future of the franchise YRF should kill the spy universe now its better to end on a high note after Tiger 3 than to drag it out with halfbaked scripts and forced entries510 watchable but nowhere near the quality of the earlier films

What I loved most about War 2 was how it constantly kept me on my toes without ever feeling like it was trying too hard The story unfolds with such confidence that every twist and turn feels earned not just thrown in for shock value Just when you think youve figured out the game it shifts direction in a way that still makes perfect sense Its not just about surprise for the sake of surprise its about letting the narrative breathe then hitting you with something that deepens your investment in the outcome By the time the climax arrives youre so caught up in it that the payoff feels incredibly satisfying

A hero is only as good as their antagonist and War 2 gives us one thats both formidable and fascinating The villain isnt just a stock bad guy he has his own code his own motivations and at times you can even understand where hes coming from That complexity makes every confrontation with the hero more than just a physical clash its a clash of ideals The tension between them builds steadily throughout the film until their final faceoff which is one of the most satisfying showdowns Ive seen in recent years You walk away not just remembering the heros triumph but also how close it all came to going the other way

Bollywood doesnt have a talent problem it has a courage problemIt loves powerful actors only as long as they stay within the invisible cage of hero worship politics The moment someone like NTR enters the frame an actor who can dominate scenes command dialogue and bend attention without begging for it the system panics So instead of writing him a villain worthy of his calibre they do what insecure industries always do they shrink the giant to make the statue look tallLook at how other villains are treated in the same socalled spy universe framed with menace dignity even admiration Then look at how NTR is handled restrained arcs clipped authority controlled presence Not because the story demanded it but because the star system couldnt afford to let him breathe freely Thats not direction thats damage control Thats not filmmaking thats political editingBollywood loves to shout about panIndia cinema but the truth is uglier they want the reach not the rivalry They want the hype of casting a powerhouse but not the risk of letting him steal the spotlight So they hire a lion and train him to roar on cue softly politely never too loud because heaven forbid the villain becomes more memorable than the hero This is not balance This is fear dressed up as strategyAnd the real humiliation for the industryEven after all this containment all this narrative neutering all this cheap politics NTR still stands out Still commands attention Still makes people talk about what could have been That alone exposes the system for what it is a place that doesnt elevate talent it manages it trims it tames it and markets the leftovers as greatnessBollywood doesnt suppress NTR because hes weakThey suppress him because hes too strong for their fragile hierarchyAnd nothing humiliates an industry more than needing to chain talent just to protect egosThats not cinemaThats insecurity with a budget
